text.title: Tuckman Team Development Stages
text.summary: Five-stage model with characteristics, PM actions, and transition signals for each stage.

section.title: Forming
section.p: Team is polite, cautious, and dependent on the PM for direction. Roles unclear, goals abstract, conflict avoided.

list.item: PM actions: clarify roles and responsibilities, set expectations, facilitate introductions, produce team charter
Transition signal: members begin asking each other questions rather than always going to the PM

section.title: Storming
section.p: Conflict, competition, and frustration surface as individuals push back on roles and process. This is healthy and expected; suppressing it delays Norming.

list.item: PM actions: facilitate structured discussion with ground rules, mediate with focus on shared goals, document agreed decisions
Transition signal: conflict becomes task-focused rather than interpersonal; throughput stabilises

section.title: Norming
section.p: Trust builds, cooperation replaces competition, norms are established. Team starts self-organising.

list.item: PM actions: delegate more, reinforce positive norms, support team decisions, encourage cross-training
Transition signal: team resolves minor issues independently without escalating to PM

section.title: Performing
section.p: High productivity, autonomy, distributed leadership. PM shifts to removing obstacles and shielding from external disruption.

list.item: PM actions: empower and trust, remove blockers, protect team from scope thrash, focus on outcomes
Transition signal: velocity or throughput is stable and predictable over multiple sprints

section.title: Adjourning
section.p: Project ends or team dissolves. Emotional closure, knowledge preservation, and individual transition support.

list.item: PM actions: celebrate achievements, conduct final retrospective, document lessons learned, facilitate career transitions
Transition signal: project closure milestone reached; team members reassigned

rule.statement: When staging a team from indirect signals, emit a confidence score (0.0-1.0) and list the evidence; do not state the stage as fact without observable data.
rule.rationale: Tuckman is a model, not a measurement instrument; overconfident staging from sparse signals leads to wrong PM actions.
